atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient reports left shoulder pain

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient reports persistent left shoulder pain for the past three weeks

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient describes the pain as dull and aching

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient describes the pain as sharp episodes

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient denies any recent injury or trauma

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient has tried over-the-counter ibuprofen

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient states minimal relief with ibuprofen

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient states 'It's really limiting my ability to do things around the house.'

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: Physical examination reveals mild tenderness to palpation over the anterior shoulder

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: Range of motion is limited by approximately 15 degrees in abduction and external rotation

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: There is no swelling or erythema

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: Neurological exam is intact, with full strength and sensation in the affected extremity

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient's presentation is most consistent with a subacute rotator cuff strain

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: Differential diagnoses include adhesive capsulitis and bursitis
